messages.content: What do you think of your digs?

Pretty great digs. The spec feels unusually coherent: the torus, slice, mode, and sigil-control ideas all snap together into a real navigation instrument instead of just a gallery UI.

What I like most in TimeLike is that playback is not a metaphor pasted on top, it actually matches the topology. The main thing I’d watch is the slight overlap between story detection, strip assignment, and time direction, because that’s where “what is time here?” could get gloriously expressive or a little confusing.Pretty great digs. The spec feels unusually coherent: the torus, slice, mode, and sigil-control ideas all snap together into a real navigation instrument instead of just a gallery UI.

What I like most in TimeLike is that playback is not a metaphor pasted on top, it actually matches the topology. The main thing I’d watch is the slight overlap between story detection, strip assignment, and time direction, because that’s where “what is time here?” could get gloriously expressive or a little confusing.

I remember the shape of this spec and what you’re looking at now. You’re in Explore > Mode > DistanceMetric, where close and far can mean embedding, time, place, or a composite, and Similarity is just the inverse of that.

More broadly, Sigil Atlas is your idea for navigating image taste on a 2D torus, with TimeLike, SpaceLike, and TasteLike as the main arrangements, and with sigil controls shaping the slice while mode shapes the layout. I also remember you asked what I thought of the digs, and I said the whole thing felt coherent and especially strong around TimeLike.
What do you think about attention language?

I think it’s the most ambitious and weirdly fertile part of the whole thing. The moment you say a sigil is recognizable by attention, named by attention, and bounded by invariants, you stop just designing UI and start designing a way the system can perceive stable patterns.

What I’d want is to keep it grounded so it earns its mysticism. Right now it feels strongest as the layer that explains how contrasts, attractors, names, and modes become legible structures rather than arbitrary controls, but it still wants one crisp operational definition of what attention actually does in the app.
